question: [File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: extractNftMultiUnionQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a request with both token.id=gte:X and token.id=lte:Y (a wide range) and no serialnumber filter, causing getLowerFilters() to include both lower and upper primary bounds in a single query that performs a wide range scan on the nft table, and when issued at scale, degrade 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getNftsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with the maximum number of spender.id=eq: values (up to maxRepeatedQueryParameters), causing spenderIdInFilters to grow large and NftService.getNfts() to execute a query with a large IN-clause on the spender column, and when issued concurrently, exhaust database CPU and degrade 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: listStakingRewardsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user issue requests to /accounts/:idOrAliasOrEvmAddress/rewards with order=asc and timestamp=gte:0, causing StakingRewardTransferService.getRewards() to perform a full ascending scan of the staking_reward_transfer table, and when issued at high concurrency, exhaust database I/O and degrade 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: extractNftMultiUnionQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with token.id=gte:0 and serialnumber=gte:0 and serialnumber=lte:MAX, causing validateBounds() to pass (since primary has lower and secondary has both lower and upper), and the resulting three-part UNION query to exhaust database resources when issued at scale, degrading 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getNftsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user issue requests with token.id=gte:0 and token.id=lte:9999999999999 (maximum token ID range) and limit=100, causing NftService.getNfts() to scan the widest possible token ID range and return maximum rows, and when issued concurrently, exhaust database memory and degrade 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: extractStakingRewardsQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user submit requests with many repeated timestamp=eq: values at the maxRepeatedQueryParameters boundary, causing updateQueryFiltersWithInValues() to build a large IN-clause and the resulting SQL to perform many point lookups in a single query, and when issued concurrently, exhaust database CPU and degrade 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getNftsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user issue requests with a valid account ID that owns a very large number of NFTs and no filters, causing NftService.getNfts() to return the maximum limit of rows with maximum data per row, and when issued at high concurrency, exhaust Node.js heap memory and degrade 30%+ of processing nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: listStakingRewardsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user issue requests to /accounts/:idOrAliasOrEvmAddress/rewards with a non-existent account alias, causing EntityService.getEncodedId() to call getAccountIdFromAlias() (DB lookup) and then isValidAccount() to return false and throw NotFoundError, and when issued at high concurrency with many distinct aliases, exhaust database connections and degrade 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: extractNftMultiUnionQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a request with token.id=lte:9999999999999 and serialnumber=lte:9999999999999, causing getUpperFilters() to generate an upper-bound sub-query and the full multi-union query to scan the widest possible range, and when issued at scale, exhaust database I/O and degrade 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getNftsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user issue requests with spender.id=lte:9999999999999 (matching all spenders) and limit=100, causing NftService.getNfts() to scan all NFTs for the account with a broad spender upper-bound filter, and when issued at high concurrency, exhaust database CPU and degrade 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: listStakingRewardsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user issue requests to /accounts/:idOrAliasOrEvmAddress/rewards with timestamp=gte:X and timestamp=lte:Y spanning a wide range, causing StakingRewardTransferService.getRewards() to scan a large portion of the staking_reward_transfer table, and when issued at high concurrency, exhaust database I/O and degrade 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: extractNftMultiUnionQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with token.id=gte:X, token.id=lte:Y, serialnumber=gte:A, and serialnumber=lte:B simultaneously, causing extractNftMultiUnionQuery() to produce lower, inner, and upper sub-queries all passed to NftService.getNfts(), generating a three-part UNION that is significantly more expensive than a single query, degrading 30%+ of nodes at scale?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getNftsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user issue requests with a valid account ID and order=desc (default) with limit=100, causing NftService.getNfts() to perform a descending full scan returning maximum rows, and when issued at high concurrency across many account IDs, exhaust database resources and degrade 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: listStakingRewardsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user issue requests to /accounts/:idOrAliasOrEvmAddress/rewards with limit=100 and no timestamp filter, causing StakingRewardTransferService.getRewards() to scan the entire staking_reward_transfer table for the account and return maximum rows, and when issued at high concurrency, exhaust database memory and degrade 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: extractNftMultiUnionQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with the maximum number of spender.id=eq: values combined with token.id=gte:0 and serialnumber=gte:0, causing extractNftMultiUnionQuery() to produce a three-part UNION query with a large IN-clause on spender, generating a highly expensive query that degrades 30%+ of nodes when issued concurrently?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getNftsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user issue requests with a shard.realm.num entity ID format (e.g., 0.0.12345) as :idOrAliasOrEvmAddress, causing EntityService.getEncodedId() to call EntityId.parseString() and getEncodedId() without a DB lookup, allowing the attacker to skip the alias resolution overhead and directly flood NftService.getNfts() with maximum-cost queries, degrading 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: listStakingRewardsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user issue requests to /accounts/:idOrAliasOrEvmAddress/rewards with a numeric account ID (no DB lookup needed for getEncodedId), bypassing the alias resolution overhead and directly flooding isValidAccount() and getRewards() with concurrent queries, exhausting database connections and degrading 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: extractStakingRewardsQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user submit requests with timestamp=gt:X and timestamp=lt:Y (open-ended range), causing updateConditionsAndParamsWithInValues() to add two range conditions to the staking_reward_transfer query, and when issued at high concurrency with wide timestamp ranges, exhaust database I/O and degrade 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getNftsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user issue requests with token.id=eq:X (a token with many NFTs) and limit=100, causing NftService.getNfts() to scan all NFTs for that token owned by the account, and when issued at high concurrency with many distinct token IDs, exhaust database CPU and degrade 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: listStakingRewardsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user issue requests to /accounts/:idOrAliasOrEvmAddress/rewards with timestamp=lte:9999999999.999999999 (far future), causing StakingRewardTransferService.getRewards() to scan the entire staking_reward_transfer table up to the maximum timestamp, and when issued at high concurrency, exhaust database I/O and degrade 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: extractNftMultiUnionQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with spender.id=gte:0 and spender.id=lte:9999999999999 (both allowed as single range operators per validateFilters()), causing NftService.getNfts() to scan all NFTs with any spender, and when issued at high concurrency, exhaust database resources and degrade 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getNftsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user issue requests with token.id=gte:0 and no serialnumber filter, causing getLowerFilters() to include only the primary lower bound and produce a query that scans all NFTs with token_id >= 0 for the account, and when issued at high concurrency, exhaust database I/O and degrade 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: listStakingRewardsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user issue requests to /accounts/:idOrAliasOrEvmAddress/rewards with order=asc and limit=100 and timestamp=gte:0, causing StakingRewardTransferService.getRewards() to perform a full ascending scan of the staking_reward_transfer table, and when issued at high concurrency, exhaust database CPU and degrade 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: extractNftMultiUnionQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with token.id=gte:X and serialnumber=gte:A (triggering the lower-bound multi-union path in getLowerFilters()), causing the query to be split into a lower sub-query and an inner sub-query, and when issued at high concurrency, exhaust database resources and degrade 30%+ of nodes?
